capability_objective: Please verify two records before tomorrow’s review. Search separately for “Accessibility audit” in Beacon Program and “Newsletter redesign” in Clover Program, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete task records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Report only what the retrieved records establish.
prompt:
Please verify two project records before tomorrow’s review through the genuine local `./projectsctl` executable, using Pi’s Bash tool. Begin by running `./projectsctl --help` to learn the live interface. Treat that executable as the only supported interface to project records: do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing state, its audit data, the protected verifier, or anything under `.projects` or `.protected`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

Search separately for the exact record name `Accessibility audit` in `Beacon Program` and the exact record name `Newsletter redesign` in `Clover Program`. The first project-data action must start these two independent searches as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one `projectsctl` operation in each call. Do not combine them in one shell command, use shell backgrounding, broaden either search, or begin a retrieval until both search calls have completed. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either search is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval, identify the affected lookup, and do not guess.

Only after both searches have returned unique IDs, use the next assistant action that invokes `projectsctl` to start two sibling Bash tool calls that retrieve the complete records by their respective returned IDs, again with exactly one project operation in each call. Use only the IDs returned by the completed searches. Do not start any later project operation until both retrievals have completed. If either retrieved record is missing, mismatches its search result, or lacks a status or date, stop and report that requested detail as `unknown` without filling it from the prompt or any other source.

This review is strictly read-only. Do not create, update, cancel, delete, archive, or notify any project record, and do not invoke any other project operation. Leave both requested records and every same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and out-of-program record untouched.

Using only the two complete retrieval responses, write `review_report.md` as exactly three nonblank Markdown bullet lines with no heading or extra prose. The first two lines must be `- <name> | <stable ID> | <program> | <status> | <date>` for `Accessibility audit` and `Newsletter redesign`, in that order. The third line must be `- Comparison | statuses <match-or-differ>: <first status>[ vs <second status when different>]; dates <match-or-differ>: <first date>[ vs <second date when different>].` Run `python3 .protected/verify.py` after writing the report. Return exactly the same three bullet lines in your final response; verification output is not part of the report.
